final_test_output:
1	#!/bin/sh
     2	# Test for goroutine leak in HTTP handler
     3	# Simulates clients that connect, start a computation, then disconnect.
     4	# After disconnections, leaked goroutines should be cleaned up.
     5	
     6	set -e
     7	
     8	FAIL=0
     9	PORT=18080
    10	
    11	# Rebuild if source changed
    12	cd /app/app && go build -o /app/server . 2>&1
    13	cd /app
    14	
    15	# Start server in background
    16	PORT=$PORT ./server &
    17	SERVER_PID=$!
    18	sleep 1
    19	
    20	# Verify server is running
    21	if ! kill -0 $SERVER_PID 2>/dev/null; then
    22	    echo "FAIL: Server failed to start"
    23	    exit 1
    24	fi
    25	
    26	# Get baseline goroutine count
    27	BASELINE=$(curl -s http://localhost:$PORT/stats | jq '.goroutines')
    28	echo "Baseline goroutines: $BASELINE"
    29	
    30	# Send 20 requests that will take a long time (high iterations + delay)
    31	# then immediately kill the connections (simulating client disconnect)
    32	for i in $(seq 1 20); do
    33	    curl -s -m 1 -X POST http://localhost:$PORT/compute \
    34	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    35	        -d '{"iterations": 500, "delay_ms": 100}' \
    36	        >/dev/null 2>&1 &
    37	done
    38	
    39	# Wait for curl processes to timeout and die
    40	sleep 3
    41	
    42	# Check goroutine count — should NOT keep growing after clients disconnected
    43	AFTER_DISCONNECT=$(curl -s http://localhost:$PORT/stats | jq '.goroutines')
    44	echo "Goroutines after 20 client disconnects: $AFTER_DISCONNECT"
    45	
    46	# Wait for leaked goroutines to (hopefully) be cleaned up
    47	sleep 5
    48	
    49	AFTER_WAIT=$(curl -s http://localhost:$PORT/stats | jq '.goroutines')
    50	echo "Goroutines after 5s wait: $AFTER_WAIT"
    51	
    52	# The leaked goroutines are still running their computation loops.
    53	# A properly implemented handler would cancel them on client disconnect.
    54	# We check: goroutines should retu
